name: workspace-formatter
description: Run configurable VS Code commands on individual files or entire folders from the Explorer context menu.

commands.title: File Commander: Run Command on File
File Commander: Run Commands on Folder

items.patternErrorMessage: Each extension must begin with a period, e.g. ".java"

fileCommander.allowedExtensions.markdownDescription: File extensions that may be targeted by the **Run Command on File** right-click action. Use dot-prefixed lowercase format — e.g. `[".java", ".cs", ".ts"]`. If left empty, all extensions are allowed.

default.label: Format Document
default.description: Apply the code formatter to the file

id.markdownDescription: The VS Code command ID to execute (e.g. `"editor.action.formatDocument"`). Find it via **Help → Show All Commands**, locate the command, and copy its ID.

label.description: Display name shown in the command picker. Supports VS Code icon syntax, e.g. "$(file-code) Format Document".

description.description: Optional subtitle shown under the label in the picker.

fileCommander.commands.markdownDescription:
Commands available in the file and folder command pickers. The order here controls the order they appear in the picker.

Example:
```json
[
  { "id": "editor.action.organizeImports", "label": "Organize Imports" },
  { "id": "editor.action.formatDocument", "label": "Format Document", "description": "Runs the active formatter" }
]
```

fileCommander.saveAfterCommands.markdownDescription: Save each file after all commands have run (only if the document was modified).

fileCommander.closeAfterProcessing.markdownDescription: Close editor tabs that were **not already open** before processing started. Files that were open before a run are never closed.
